schoolName: University of Calgary (Cumming School of Medicine)
reviewNotes: HOLD - do not publish. Cumming School of Medicine MD program admissions are restricted by official policy to Canadian citizens, permanent residents of Canada, and Convention refugees in Canada; international (including Indian) students are explicitly not eligible to apply (https://cumming.ucalgary.ca/mdprogram/future-students/admissions/am-i-eligible, corroborated by UCalgary Calendar 5.3.1 and BeMo Academic Consulting summary). The school does not offer BDS, Bachelor of Pharmacy, or BSc Nursing degrees. Its Graduate Science Education (PhD/MSc) and Postgraduate Medical Education (residency/fellowship for already-licensed physicians via AIMG/sponsored pathways) divisions do not map to this site's mbbs/medical-pg/bsc-nursing/pharmacy/bds courseSlug taxonomy for India-outbound undergraduate/postgraduate study-abroad seekers. No program entry is included because none can be honestly offered to this audience. See the accompanying .md research record for full source list and details.

structuredFacts.name: University of Calgary (Cumming School of Medicine)
structuredFacts.notes: GATE NOT MET: no program with an allowed courseSlug (mbbs, medical-pg, bsc-nursing, pharmacy, bds) can be honestly listed. MD program is closed to international students by official policy (Canadian citizens/PRs/Convention refugees only). No BDS/Pharmacy/Nursing degrees offered by this school.

thingsToConsider: The MD Program at the Cumming School of Medicine is open only to Canadian citizens, permanent residents of Canada, and Convention refugees in Canada -- international students, including Indian applicants, are not eligible to apply.
Canadian medical schools use a graduate-entry MD model, not an undergraduate MBBS-style route, so this school cannot be compared directly to MBBS-abroad destinations.
The university does not offer BDS, Bachelor of Pharmacy, or BSc Nursing degree programs, so it does not fit the site's other India-outbound course categories either.
